project.name: chrome-extension-boilerplate-react-vite
project.description: A Chrome extension boilerplate and new-tab redirector built with React, TypeScript, Vite, and Turborepo, supporting Manifest V3 with HMR, i18n, and e2e testing.

nodes.name: copy_env.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that copies .example.env to .env if .env does not already exist, providing safe initial environment setup for developers.

nodes.name: set_global_env.sh
nodes.summary: CLI-driven script that parses CLI arguments (CLI_CEB_DEV, CLI_CEB_FIREFOX, etc.), validates env keys against naming conventions, and rewrites the .env file separating CLI-managed and user-editable sections.

nodes.name: update_version.sh
nodes.summary: Shell script that updates the version field across all package.json files in the monorepo, validating semver format (X.Y.Z) and using perl for in-place replacement.

nodes.name: config.ts
nodes.summary: Centralized configuration module for the bridge server, re-exporting protocol constants (BRIDGE_PORT, CDP_PORT) and defining derived filesystem paths (DATA_DIR, COMMANDS_FILE, LOG_FILE, PID_FILE) rooted in the user's home directory.

nodes.name: devtools.ts
nodes.summary: Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP) integration that discovers browser targets, opens the extensions page if needed, and reloads an unpacked extension by evaluating chrome.developerPrivate.reload() via a CDP WebSocket connection.

nodes.name: index.ts (bridge entry)
nodes.summary: Entry point for the bridge server that creates a shared HTTP + WebSocket server on localhost, writes a PID file for process management, wires up route handlers and WebSocket connection handling, and implements graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM.

nodes.name: logger.ts
nodes.summary: Simple logging utility that writes timestamped messages to both stderr and a persistent log file (bridge.log), used throughout the bridge server.

nodes.name: routes.ts
nodes.summary: HTTP route handler for the bridge server implementing REST endpoints: /health (status), /commands (list), /execute (relay command to Chrome via WS), /reload-extension (CDP with WS fallback), /dev/build (trigger pnpm build), and /search-bookmarks (relay to Chrome).

nodes.name: state.ts
nodes.summary: In-memory state management for the bridge server, holding the command registry, Chrome WebSocket reference, extension ID, and synced timestamp. Provides functions for Chrome communication (send/request with timeout), pending request resolution, and command persistence to disk.

nodes.name: websocket.ts
nodes.summary: WebSocket connection handler for the bridge server that processes incoming messages from the Chrome extension (SYNC_COMMANDS, RESPONSE, PING, COMMAND_RESULT) and delegates to state management functions for command syncing and request resolution.

nodes.name: manifest.ts
nodes.summary: Generates the Chrome extension manifest.json configuration object (Manifest V3) including permissions, entry points for popup/background/content scripts/devtools/side-panel, keyboard shortcuts, and i18n settings. Reads version from package.json.

nodes.name: background/index.ts
nodes.summary: Background service worker for the Chrome extension handling WebSocket bridge connectivity with auto-reconnect, bookmark search, command execution, Chrome keyboard shortcuts, and message routing between content scripts and the bridge.

nodes.name: updateBadge
nodes.summary: Sets the extension badge text and color to indicate WebSocket bridge connection status.

nodes.name: connectBridge
nodes.summary: Establishes and manages the WebSocket connection to the local bridge server, handling message dispatch (reload, execute command, bookmark search), keep-alive pings, and auto-reconnect on disconnection.

nodes.name: searchBookmarks
nodes.summary: Searches Chrome bookmarks by query, resolves parent folder names in parallel, and returns up to 20 results with folder context.

nodes.name: executeCommand
nodes.summary: Dispatches named commands (open-options, toggle-theme) to their respective Chrome API or storage actions.

nodes.name: make-manifest-plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Vite plugin that generates manifest.json from the TypeScript manifest definition during build, handles cache-busting for hot reloads, injects HMR refresh content script in dev mode, and writes the output to the build directory.

nodes.name: withHMRId
nodes.summary: Wraps HMR refresh code in an IIFE with a HMR identifier variable.

nodes.name: getManifestWithCacheBurst
nodes.summary: Dynamically imports the compiled manifest.js with a timestamp query to bypass module cache, handling Windows file:// protocol conversion.

nodes.name: addRefreshContentScript
nodes.summary: Appends a refresh.js content script entry to the manifest for HMR support during development.

nodes.name: eslint.config.ts
nodes.summary: Root ESLint flat config combining TypeScript, React, JSX accessibility, import, and Prettier plugins with project-wide rules including consistent type imports and build directory ignores.

nodes.name: logger.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a colorLog utility function for printing color-coded console messages (success/info/error/warning) using ANSI escape codes, used across build tooling.

nodes.name: colorLog
nodes.summary: Logs a message to console with an ANSI color based on the specified type (success, info, error, warning, or a direct COLORS key).

nodes.name: manifest-parser/impl.ts
nodes.summary: Implements ManifestParserInterface with logic to serialize a Chrome manifest to JSON and convert it to Firefox-compatible format by transforming background workers to scripts, options_page to options_ui, and filtering unsupported permissions.

nodes.name: convertToFirefoxCompatibleManifest
nodes.summary: Transforms a Chrome Manifest V3 object into a Firefox-compatible version by converting service_worker to background scripts, options_page to options_ui, adding CSP, and removing sidePanel permission.

nodes.name: manifest-parser/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file that re-exports ManifestParserImpl as ManifestParser along with all types from the manifest-parser module.

nodes.name: manifest-parser/types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the Manifest type alias (chrome.runtime.ManifestV3) and ManifestParserInterface with a convertManifestToString method for the manifest parser module.

nodes.name: ManifestParserInterface
nodes.summary: Interface defining the contract for manifest parsers with a method to convert a manifest object to a JSON string with optional Firefox compatibility.

nodes.name: const.ts
nodes.summary: Exports environment flag constants (IS_DEV, IS_PROD, IS_FIREFOX, IS_CI) derived from process.env variables, used across the build system and extension code.

nodes.name: env/index.ts
nodes.summary: Loads environment variables from the project root .env file using dotenvx and exports baseEnv and dynamicEnvValues (with computed CEB_NODE_ENV based on dev flag).

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Defines TypeScript interfaces for environment variables (ICebEnv, ICebCliEnv) and exports the combined IEnv type that merges static and dynamic env values.

nodes.name: consts.ts
nodes.summary: Defines HMR WebSocket constants including socket port (8081), socket URL, and message type strings (DO_UPDATE, DONE_UPDATE, BUILD_COMPLETE) used across the HMR system.

nodes.name: initClient.ts
nodes.summary: Initializes a WebSocket client that connects to the HMR reload server, listens for DO_UPDATE messages matching its id, and triggers an onUpdate callback followed by a DONE_UPDATE acknowledgment.

nodes.name: initReloadServer.ts
nodes.summary: Creates and runs a WebSocketServer on the HMR port that tracks connected clients, forwards BUILD_COMPLETE messages as DO_UPDATE to all clients, and closes connections after receiving DONE_UPDATE confirmations.

nodes.name: refresh.ts
nodes.summary: Injectable HMR script that triggers window.location.reload() on update, deferring reload until the tab becomes visible if the page is hidden to avoid unnecessary reloads.

nodes.name: reload.ts
nodes.summary: Injectable HMR script that triggers chrome.runtime.reload() to reload the Chrome extension runtime when an update is received from the HMR server.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a MessageInterpreter object with send (serializes WebSocketMessage to JSON string) and receive (deserializes JSON string to WebSocketMessage) methods for HMR protocol messaging.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file that aggregates all HMR Vite plugins: watch-rebuild-plugin, make-entry-point-plugin, and watch-public-plugin.

nodes.name: make-entry-point-plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Vite plugin that creates cache-busting entry point files for content scripts by generating _dev.js variants during build, with special handling for Firefox using browser.runtime.getURL.

nodes.name: watch-public-plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Vite plugin that watches all files in the public directory using fast-glob and adds them as watch targets to trigger rebuilds when public assets change.

nodes.name: makeEntryPointPlugin
nodes.summary: Returns a Vite PluginOption that intercepts generated bundles, creates _dev.js cache-busting copies of content script chunks, rewrites original chunks to import the copies, and cleans up leftover source maps.

nodes.name: extractContentDir
nodes.summary: Extracts the content directory path relative to dist/ from an output directory string for Firefox extension URL construction.

nodes.name: watchPublicPlugin
nodes.summary: Returns a Vite PluginOption with a buildStart hook that globs all public/** files and adds them as watch files for rebuild triggering.

nodes.name: reload
nodes.summary: Resets the pendingReload flag and triggers window.location.reload() for a full page refresh.

nodes.name: reloadWhenTabIsVisible
nodes.summary: Visibility change handler that triggers a deferred reload when the tab becomes visible and a reload was pending.

nodes.name: watch-rebuild-plugin.ts
nodes.summary: Vite plugin that watches for rebuild events and communicates with the HMR reload server via WebSocket. Injects HMR refresh/reload code into bundled chunks and signals build completion.

nodes.name: watchRebuildPlugin
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates a Vite plugin for HMR watch-rebuild. Establishes WebSocket connection to reload server with retry logic, injects HMR code into chunks via generateBundle, and sends BUILD_COMPLETE messages on writeBundle.

nodes.name: initializeWebSocket
nodes.summary: Inner function that creates a WebSocket connection to the local reload server with up to 3 reconnect attempts on error, logging connection status.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the HMR package, including WebSocket message types (UpdateRequest, UpdateComplete, BuildCompletion) and the PluginConfig type used to configure HMR plugins.

nodes.name: rollup.config.ts
nodes.summary: Rollup configuration for building the HMR injection scripts (reload.ts and refresh.ts) into ESM format JS files using the sucrase TypeScript transform plugin.

nodes.name: consts.ts
nodes.summary: Constants for the i18n package: a comprehensive map of all Chrome extension supported language codes to their display names (SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES), and the I18N_FILE_PATH pointing to the generated i18n module.

nodes.name: i18n-dev.ts
nodes.summary: Development-mode i18n translation function that reads locale JSON directly, performs placeholder substitution mimicking Chrome's i18n API, and supports numbered substitution parameters.

nodes.name: translate
nodes.summary: Core translation function for dev mode. Looks up a message key in the locale JSON, replaces named placeholders with their content values, then handles numbered substitution parameters from the substitutions argument.

nodes.name: i18n-prod.ts
nodes.summary: Production-mode i18n translation function that delegates directly to chrome.i18n.getMessage, the native Chrome extension internationalization API.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Package entry point for the i18n module. Re-exports the translation function 't' from a generated i18n.js file (which is either i18n-dev or i18n-prod depending on build), cast to the dev type signature for full type safety.

nodes.name: prepare_build.ts
nodes.summary: Build preparation script for i18n. Copies the appropriate i18n module (dev or prod) to lib/i18n.ts based on IS_DEV flag, copies locale files to the dist/_locales directory, and in dev mode adjusts locale imports to match the system locale.

nodes.name: set_related_locale_import.ts
nodes.summary: Dev-time utility that detects the system locale and rewrites the import statement in the generated i18n.ts file to point to the matching locale JSON. Falls back to the base locale (without region) or English if no exact match found. Supports CEB_DEV_LOCALE env override.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the i18n package. Derives supported language key types from the SUPPORTED_LANGUAGES constant, defines the I18nValueType for locale message entries with placeholders, and extracts MessageKey and LocalesJSONType from the English messages JSON schema.

nodes.name: deleteModules.ts
nodes.summary: Module manager tool that archives and deletes extension features (pages) from the project. Provides interactive CLI to select features to remove, zips them for recovery, and updates the manifest.

nodes.name: deleteModules
nodes.summary: Main async function that prompts the user to select extension features for deletion, then archives each selected feature folder and removes it from the manifest. Default export.

nodes.name: deleteBackgroundScript
nodes.summary: Removes the background service worker entry from the manifest object.

nodes.name: deleteContentScript
nodes.summary: Archives the content page folder to zip and removes its content_scripts entry from the manifest.

nodes.name: deleteContentScriptUI
nodes.summary: Archives the content-ui page folder to zip and removes its content_scripts entry from the manifest.

nodes.name: deleteContentScriptRuntime
nodes.summary: Archives the content-runtime page folder to zip and removes its content_scripts entry from the manifest.

nodes.name: deleteNewTabOverride
nodes.summary: Archives the new-tab page folder and removes chrome_url_overrides.newtab from manifest.

nodes.name: deletePopup
nodes.summary: Archives the popup page folder and removes action.default_popup from manifest.

nodes.name: deleteDevTools
nodes.summary: Archives both devtools and devtools-panel page folders and removes devtools_page from manifest.

nodes.name: deleteSidePanel
nodes.summary: Archives the side-panel page folder, removes side_panel from manifest, and removes the sidePanel permission.

nodes.name: deleteOptionsPage
nodes.summary: Archives the options page folder and removes options_page from manifest.

nodes.name: zipFolder
nodes.summary: Async utility that creates a zip archive of a folder using fflate streaming compression. Returns a promise that resolves when the archive is complete.

nodes.name: streamFileToZip
nodes.summary: Streams a single file into an in-progress zip archive using fflate's AsyncZipDeflate for async compression.

nodes.name: recoverModules.ts
nodes.summary: Module manager tool that recovers previously deleted extension features from zip archives. Provides interactive CLI to select features to restore, unzips them, and re-adds manifest entries.

nodes.name: recoverModules
nodes.summary: Main async function that prompts the user to select archived features for recovery, unzips them back into the pages directory, and restores their manifest entries. Default export.

nodes.name: recoverBackgroundScript
nodes.summary: Restores the background service worker entry in the manifest.

nodes.name: recoverContentScript
nodes.summary: Restores the content script content_scripts manifest entry and unzips the archived content folder.

nodes.name: recoverContentScriptUI
nodes.summary: Restores the content-ui content_scripts manifest entry and unzips the archived content-ui folder.

nodes.name: recoverContentScriptRuntime
nodes.summary: Restores the content-runtime content_scripts manifest entry and unzips the archived content-runtime folder.

nodes.name: recoverNewTabOverride
nodes.summary: Restores chrome_url_overrides.newtab in manifest and unzips the new-tab folder.

nodes.name: recoverPopup
nodes.summary: Restores the popup action entry in manifest and unzips the popup folder.

nodes.name: recoverDevTools
nodes.summary: Restores devtools_page in manifest and unzips both devtools and devtools-panel folders.

nodes.name: recoverSidePanel
nodes.summary: Restores side_panel in manifest, adds sidePanel permission, and unzips the side-panel folder.

nodes.name: recoverOptionsPage
nodes.summary: Restores options_page in manifest and unzips the options folder.

nodes.name: upZipAndDelete
nodes.summary: Utility that synchronously unzips an archive to a destination directory and deletes the zip file.

nodes.name: runModuleManager.ts
nodes.summary: Entry point for the module manager CLI tool. Reads the chrome extension manifest, prompts the user to choose delete or recover, executes the action, then writes the updated manifest and runs eslint fix and pnpm install.

nodes.name: runModuleManager
nodes.summary: Main orchestrator function for the module manager. Presents a delete/recover choice, delegates to the corresponding module, writes updated manifest, runs eslint --fix, and pnpm install.

nodes.name: index.ts (protocol)
nodes.summary: Shared protocol package defining network constants (ports, URLs), domain types (CommandDef, BookmarkResult), Chrome extension internal message types, and bridge WebSocket protocol message types shared across bridge, extension, and Raycast.

nodes.name: CommandDef
nodes.summary: Defines a command that can be executed by the extension, command palette, or Raycast. Has id, label, optional description, and context (background or content).

nodes.name: BookmarkResult
nodes.summary: A bookmark search result returned by the extension with id, title, url, and optional folder.

nodes.name: ExtensionMessage
nodes.summary: Union type for Chrome extension internal messages sent via chrome.runtime.sendMessage. Includes EXECUTE_COMMAND, OPEN_OPTIONS_PAGE, TOGGLE_COMMAND_PALETTE, and SEARCH_BOOKMARKS.

nodes.name: ExtensionResponseMap
nodes.summary: Maps each ExtensionMessage type to its expected response type.

nodes.name: BridgeUpMessage
nodes.summary: Union type for messages sent from the extension to the bridge over WebSocket. Includes SYNC_COMMANDS, PING, RESPONSE, and COMMAND_RESULT.

nodes.name: BridgeDownMessage
nodes.summary: Union type for messages sent from the bridge to the extension over WebSocket. Includes SYNC_ACK, PONG, EXECUTE_COMMAND, SEARCH_BOOKMARKS, and RELOAD_EXTENSION.

nodes.name: index.ts (hoc)
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file for the shared higher-order components package. Re-exports withSuspense and withErrorBoundary.

nodes.name: withErrorBoundary.tsx
nodes.summary: Provides a React higher-order component that wraps a component with an ErrorBoundary class component. Catches rendering errors and displays a fallback UI.

nodes.name: ErrorBoundary
nodes.summary: React class component implementing error boundary pattern. Extends Component with children/fallback props and hasError state. Uses getDerivedStateFromError and componentDidCatch lifecycle methods.

nodes.name: withErrorBoundary
nodes.summary: Higher-order component that wraps a given component inside an ErrorBoundary, providing error catching with a custom fallback element.

nodes.name: WithErrorBoundary
nodes.summary: Inner wrapper component returned by withErrorBoundary HOC. Renders the wrapped component inside an ErrorBoundary.

nodes.name: withSuspense.tsx
nodes.summary: Provides a React higher-order component that wraps a component with React Suspense, showing a fallback UI during lazy loading.

nodes.name: withSuspense
nodes.summary: Higher-order component that wraps a given component inside React Suspense with a custom fallback element for lazy loading.

nodes.name: WithSuspense
nodes.summary: Inner wrapper component returned by withSuspense HOC. Renders the wrapped component inside React Suspense.

nodes.name: index.ts (hooks)
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file for the shared hooks package. Re-exports everything from useStorage.

nodes.name: useStorage.tsx
nodes.summary: React hook for subscribing to Chrome extension storage. Uses useSyncExternalStore for synchronous reads and wrapPromise for Suspense-compatible initial loading. Caches storage subscriptions in a module-level Map.

nodes.name: useStorage
nodes.summary: React hook that subscribes to a BaseStorage instance using useSyncExternalStore. Supports Suspense by throwing a wrapped promise on initial load. Returns typed storage data.

nodes.name: wrapPromise
nodes.summary: Utility that wraps a promise for React Suspense integration. Returns an object with a read() method that throws the promise while pending, throws the error on rejection, or returns the value on success.

nodes.name: index.ts (utils)
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file for the shared utils package. Re-exports everything from shared-types.

nodes.name: shared-types.ts
nodes.summary: Exports a single generic utility type ValueOf<T> that extracts the union of all value types from an object type T. A minimal shared type helper used across the monorepo.

nodes.name: base.ts
nodes.summary: Core storage factory for Chrome extension storage areas. The createStorage function wraps chrome.storage API with a cache layer, supports get/set/subscribe/getSnapshot, handles session access levels, serialization, and optional live updates via onChanged listener.

nodes.name: createStorage
nodes.summary: Factory function that creates a BaseStorage<D> instance backed by a chrome.storage area (local/sync/session/managed). Manages an in-memory cache, supports serialization/deserialization, live update listeners, and session access level configuration.

nodes.name: updateCache
nodes.summary: Helper that resolves a ValueOrUpdate<D> (either a direct value or an updater function) against the current cache, supporting both sync and async updaters.

nodes.name: checkStoragePermission
nodes.summary: Validates that the specified chrome.storage area is available, throwing if the required storage permission is missing from manifest.json.

nodes.name: enums.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the StorageEnum (Local, Sync, Managed, Session) mapping to Chrome storage area names, and SessionAccessLevelEnum for controlling content script access to session storage.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file for the storage base module. Re-exports everything from base.ts, enums.ts, and types.ts.

nodes.name: types.ts
nodes.summary: Type definitions for the storage system: ValueOrUpdate<D> (value or updater function), BaseStorage<D> (get/set/subscribe/getSnapshot interface), and StorageConfig<D> (storageEnum, liveUpdate, sessionAccess, serialization options).

nodes.name: commandPaletteShortcutStorage.ts
nodes.summary: Storage implementation for the command palette keyboard shortcut. Stores a ShortcutConfig (key, ctrlOrMeta, shift, alt) in Chrome sync storage with live updates. Extends BaseStorage with setShortcut and reset convenience methods.

nodes.name: commandRegistry.ts
nodes.summary: Defines the static command registry array listing available extension commands (open-options, toggle-theme, search-bookmarks) with their IDs, labels, descriptions, and execution contexts. Re-exports the CommandDef type from @extension/protocol.

nodes.name: exampleThemeStorage.ts
nodes.summary: Storage implementation for theme state (light/dark). Uses Chrome sync storage with live updates and extends BaseStorage with a toggle() convenience method.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file for all storage implementations: exampleThemeStorage, weightedUrlsStorage, themePresetStorage, commandRegistry, commandPaletteShortcutStorage, and messages.

nodes.name: messages.ts
nodes.summary: Provides a type-safe sendExtensionMessage wrapper around chrome.runtime.sendMessage. Re-exports ExtensionMessage, ExtensionResponseMap, and BookmarkResult types from @extension/protocol. Includes a deprecated MessageResponseMap alias.

nodes.name: sendExtensionMessage
nodes.summary: Type-safe wrapper around chrome.runtime.sendMessage that infers the response type from the ExtensionResponseMap based on the message type.

nodes.name: themePresetStorage.ts
nodes.summary: Defines theme preset storage with four named color presets (sunset-horizon, catppuccin, claymorphism, vintage-paper), each containing full light/dark oklch color palettes. Provides storage for persisting the active preset and a utility to apply CSS custom properties to the DOM.

nodes.name: weightedUrlsStorage.ts
nodes.summary: Implements weighted URL storage for tab redirection. Supports CRUD operations on URL entries with probability weights, weighted random URL selection, URL protocol normalization, and migration from a legacy plain-string format.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Package barrel that re-exports BaseStorage type from base module and all implementation storage instances from impl module.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Top-level barrel for the UI package, re-exporting everything from lib/index.

nodes.name: ToggleButton.tsx
nodes.summary: React button component that toggles the example theme storage between light/dark. Uses useStorage hook for reactive state and cn utility for conditional Tailwind class merging.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel file that re-exports all UI components: ToggleButton, Button, Tooltip, Separator, and AppSidebar.

nodes.name: app-sidebar.tsx
nodes.summary: Discord-style vertical icon sidebar with active-state pill indicator, hover tooltips via pure CSS, and an AppLayout wrapper providing sidebar+main flex layout. Designed for multi-app navigation.

nodes.name: button.tsx
nodes.summary: Shadcn-style Button component using class-variance-authority for variant/size props (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) with forwarded ref support.

nodes.name: separator.tsx
nodes.summary: Lightweight separator component supporting horizontal/vertical orientation with optional ARIA separator role. Replaces Radix dependency with a plain div.

nodes.name: tooltip.tsx
nodes.summary: Custom tooltip system with Provider, Tooltip, Trigger, and Content components using React context for open state management. Supports configurable delay, four positioning sides, and replaces Radix tooltip dependency.

nodes.name: packages/ui/lib/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export file for the UI package; re-exports all components and utilities from sub-modules.

nodes.name: ThemeColors
nodes.summary: Interface defining the complete set of CSS color tokens (background, foreground, card, primary, secondary, muted, accent, destructive, border, input, ring, chart-1..5, radius, sidebar variants) used by theme presets.

nodes.name: ThemePreset
nodes.summary: Interface for a named theme preset containing a label and separate light/dark ThemeColors configurations.

nodes.name: applyThemePreset
nodes.summary: Applies a named theme preset's CSS custom properties to a DOM root element for a given light/dark mode.

nodes.name: ensureUrlHasProtocol
nodes.summary: Prepends https:// to URLs that lack a protocol prefix.

nodes.name: WeightedUrl
nodes.summary: Interface for a URL entry with a probability weight for weighted random selection.

nodes.name: ToggleButton
nodes.summary: React component that renders a themed button toggling the exampleThemeStorage on click.

nodes.name: AppSidebar
nodes.summary: Vertical icon sidebar component rendering a list of apps with active state indicators, optional header/footer slots, and CSS-only hover tooltips.

nodes.name: SidebarItem
nodes.summary: Individual sidebar icon button with active pill indicator and hover tooltip. Internal to app-sidebar module.

nodes.name: AppLayout
nodes.summary: Full-screen flex layout wrapper that places a sidebar alongside a scrollable main content area.

nodes.name: Button
nodes.summary: Forwarded-ref button component with CVA-powered variant (default, destructive, outline, secondary, ghost, link) and size (default, sm, lg, icon) props.

nodes.name: Separator
nodes.summary: Forwarded-ref visual divider component supporting horizontal/vertical orientation with ARIA role.

nodes.name: Tooltip
nodes.summary: Context provider component managing open/close state with configurable delay for tooltip display.

nodes.name: TooltipTrigger
nodes.summary: Forwarded-ref button that opens the tooltip on hover (with delay) and focus, closes on leave and blur.

nodes.name: TooltipContent
nodes.summary: Positioned tooltip content rendered conditionally based on open state, supports top/right/bottom/left side placement.

nodes.name: packages/ui/lib/utils.ts
nodes.summary: Utility module providing the `cn` function that merges Tailwind CSS class names using clsx and tailwind-merge.

nodes.name: cn
nodes.summary: Merges class name values using clsx for conditional classes and tailwind-merge for deduplicating Tailwind utilities.

nodes.name: packages/vite-config/lib/index.ts
nodes.summary: Barrel re-export for the vite-config package; re-exports everything from withPageConfig.

nodes.name: packages/vite-config/lib/withPageConfig.ts
nodes.summary: Shared Vite configuration factory for extension pages. Provides `withPageConfig` that merges a base config (React SWC, Tailwind, HMR, source maps, env vars) with page-specific overrides via deepmerge.

nodes.name: withPageConfig
nodes.summary: Creates a Vite config by deep-merging a standard base config (React SWC, Tailwind CSS, HMR watch-rebuild, env define, build options) with a page-specific UserConfig override.

nodes.name: packages/zipper/lib/index.ts
nodes.summary: Build artifact zipping utility. Uses fflate for async streaming compression and fast-glob for file discovery to create zip archives of the dist directory, optionally excluding source maps.

nodes.name: zipBundle
nodes.summary: Async function that zips a dist directory into a compressed archive. Uses fast-glob for file discovery and fflate for streaming deflate compression. Optionally excludes source maps.

nodes.name: toMB
nodes.summary: Converts byte count to megabytes.

nodes.name: ensureBuildDirectoryExists
nodes.summary: Creates the build output directory recursively if it does not exist.

nodes.name: logPackageSize
nodes.summary: Logs the final zip package size in MB and the elapsed time.

nodes.name: streamFileToZip
nodes.summary: Streams a single file into the zip archive using fflate AsyncZipDeflate with max compression level.

nodes.name: pages/content-runtime/src/App.tsx
nodes.summary: Minimal React component for the content-runtime page. Renders a simple div and logs on mount.

nodes.name: App
nodes.summary: Default-exported React component for the content-runtime view. Logs load event on mount and renders a static div.

nodes.name: pages/content-runtime/src/Root.tsx
nodes.summary: Mount logic for the content-runtime page. Creates a shadow DOM root, injects inline CSS (with Firefox compatibility), and renders the App component using React createRoot.

nodes.name: mount
nodes.summary: Creates a shadow DOM container in document.body, injects styles (using adoptedStyleSheets or a style element for Firefox), and renders the App component via React createRoot.

nodes.name: pages/content-runtime/src/index.ts
nodes.summary: Entry point for the content-runtime content script. Imports and calls mount() from Root, logs load confirmation.

nodes.name: pages/content-ui/src/App.tsx
nodes.summary: Command palette overlay UI injected into web pages via content script. Features two modes: command search (filtering registered commands) and bookmark search (async search via extension messaging). Includes keyboard navigation, theming via storage-backed presets, configurable keyboard shortcuts, debounced search, favicon display, and inline SVG icons. Largest file in the batch at 611 lines.

nodes.name: App (content-ui)
nodes.summary: Main command palette React component. Manages open/close state, dual modes (commands/bookmarks), keyboard navigation (ArrowUp/Down, Enter, Escape, Backspace), theme token generation from presets, search filtering, debounced bookmark search via extension messaging, and renders a fixed overlay with backdrop blur.

nodes.name: useDebounce
nodes.summary: Custom React hook that debounces a value by a given millisecond delay using setTimeout.

nodes.name: getTokens
nodes.summary: Derives UI design tokens (colors, shadows) from a ThemeColors preset and dark/light mode flag for the command palette.

nodes.name: faviconUrl
nodes.summary: Generates a Google favicon service URL for a given page URL by extracting its hostname.

nodes.name: SearchIcon
nodes.summary: SVG icon component rendering a search/magnifying glass icon.

nodes.name: BookmarkIcon
nodes.summary: SVG icon component rendering a bookmark icon.

nodes.name: BackIcon
nodes.summary: SVG icon component rendering a back/chevron-left icon.

nodes.name: FolderIcon
nodes.summary: SVG icon component rendering a folder icon.

nodes.name: pages/content-ui/src/index.tsx
nodes.summary: Entry point for the content-ui content script. Creates a shadow DOM root, injects Tailwind CSS (with Firefox adoptedStyleSheets workaround), and renders the App command palette component.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: Content script that relays TOGGLE_COMMAND_PALETTE messages from the Chrome runtime to the page via CustomEvent, enabling content-ui to pick them up.

nodes.name: Panel.tsx
nodes.summary: React component for the DevTools panel page. Displays a themed logo, GitHub link, and theme toggle button wrapped with error boundary and suspense HOCs.

nodes.name: Panel
nodes.summary: Functional component rendering the devtools panel UI with theme-aware logo and toggle button.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Entry point for the DevTools panel page. Mounts the Panel React component into the #app-container DOM element.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes the React root and renders the Panel component into the DOM.

nodes.name: index.ts
nodes.summary: DevTools entry point that creates a Chrome DevTools panel named 'Dev Tools' pointing to devtools-panel/index.html.

nodes.name: NewTab.tsx
nodes.summary: React component for the new-tab override page. Displays themed logo, GitHub link, and theme toggle wrapped with error boundary and suspense HOCs.

nodes.name: NewTab
nodes.summary: Functional component rendering the new-tab page with theme-aware logo and toggle button.

nodes.name: index.tsx
nodes.summary: Entry point for the new-tab page. Mounts the NewTab React component into the #app-container DOM element.

nodes.name: init
nodes.summary: Initializes the React root and renders the NewTab component into the DOM.

nodes.name: Options.tsx
nodes.summary: Main options page component with sidebar navigation (AppSidebar), secondary tab navigation (Redirector/Organizer/Bulk), theme preset application, and settings view. Acts as the layout shell for all tab management features.

nodes.name: Options
nodes.summary: Main options page component managing active view (tab-manager vs settings) and active sub-tab (redirector/organizer/bulk) with theme preset application via useEffect.

nodes.name: FooterButton
nodes.summary: Reusable sidebar footer button with tooltip on hover, used for theme toggle and settings navigation.

nodes.name: NavButton
nodes.summary: Secondary sidebar navigation button with active state styling for tab manager sub-pages.

nodes.name: TabManagerIcon
nodes.summary: SVG icon component for the Tab Manager sidebar item.

nodes.name: SettingsIcon
nodes.summary: SVG icon component for the Settings sidebar footer button.

nodes.name: ThemeIcon
nodes.summary: SVG icon component that renders sun (light) or moon (dark) based on current theme.

nodes.name: BulkActions.tsx
nodes.summary: Complex tab management component providing bulk operations: search/filter tabs (plain text or regex), select/deselect, suspend, close, archive to bookmarks, and regex-based URL renaming. Uses Chrome tabs and bookmarks APIs.

nodes.name: BulkActions
nodes.summary: Main bulk actions component managing tab loading, filtering (plain/regex), selection, suspension, closing, archiving to bookmarks, and URL renaming via regex.

nodes.name: DuplicateTabsList.tsx
nodes.summary: Component that scans all open tabs, groups them by URL to find duplicates, and provides actions to close individual duplicates, per-group duplicates, or all duplicates at once.

nodes.name: DuplicateTabsList
nodes.summary: Component that scans tabs for duplicate URLs, groups them, and provides close actions for individual tabs, per-group duplicates, or all duplicates.

nodes.name: OldTabsList.tsx
nodes.summary: Component that finds the N oldest/least-recently-accessed tabs, sorted by lastAccessed timestamp, with configurable count and close actions (individual or all).

nodes.name: OldTabsList
nodes.summary: Component that scans for old tabs sorted by last access time, with configurable count limit and close actions.

nodes.name: formatTimeAgo
nodes.summary: Utility function converting milliseconds to human-readable relative time string (days/hours/minutes ago).

nodes.name: formatDate
nodes.summary: Utility function formatting a timestamp into a localized date string.

nodes.name: Settings.tsx
nodes.summary: Settings page component for the options UI. Allows toggling light/dark mode, choosing theme presets with color swatches, and recording/resetting the command palette keyboard shortcut.

nodes.name: formatShortcut
nodes.summary: Formats a ShortcutConfig object into a human-readable shortcut string (e.g. 'Ctrl + Shift + K'), detecting Mac vs other platforms for Cmd/Ctrl labeling.

nodes.name: Settings
nodes.summary: React component for extension settings: appearance (light/dark toggle, theme presets) and keyboard shortcut recording for the command palette.

nodes.name: SuspiciousTabsList.tsx
nodes.summary: Component that scans open browser tabs to find 'suspicious' ones (domain-only URLs with no path/params). Provides ability to close individual or all suspicious tabs.

nodes.name: isSuspiciousUrl
nodes.summary: Checks whether a URL is suspicious by verifying it has no path (just domain root), no search params, and no hash.

nodes.name: SuspiciousTabsList
nodes.summary: React component that scans for and displays domain-only tabs, with individual and bulk close functionality including animated deletion.

nodes.name: TabExportImport.tsx
nodes.summary: Full-featured tab export/import component. Exports all tabs with metadata (groups, pins, windows) to JSON and imports them back in configurable batches with tab suspension to save memory.

nodes.name: TabExportImport
nodes.summary: React component providing tab export to JSON (with full metadata) and batch import with configurable batch size, batch delay, and suspend delay. Preserves tab groups and window organization.

nodes.name: TabOrganizer.tsx
nodes.summary: Container component with tabbed navigation for tab management sub-views: duplicates, suspicious, old/unused, and export/import.

nodes.name: TabOrganizer
nodes.summary: React component providing a tabbed interface that switches between DuplicateTabsList, SuspiciousTabsList, OldTabsList, and TabExportImport views.

nodes.name: TabRedirector.tsx
nodes.summary: New Tab URL Redirector settings component. Manages a list of weighted URLs that are randomly selected when a new tab is opened.

nodes.name: TabRedirector
nodes.summary: React component for managing weighted URL list for new tab redirection. Supports add, edit, delete operations on URLs with weight values via weightedUrlsStorage.

nodes.name: index.tsx (options)
nodes.summary: Entry point for the options page. Creates a React root and renders the Options component into #app-container.

nodes.name: init (options)
nodes.summary: Initializes the options page React app by finding #app-container and calling createRoot/render with the Options component.

nodes.name: Popup.tsx
nodes.summary: Popup page component showing extension logo, a button to inject content scripts into the active tab, and a theme toggle. Wrapped with error boundary and suspense.

nodes.name: Popup
nodes.summary: React component for the extension popup. Displays branding, a content script injection button using chrome.scripting.executeScript, and a ToggleButton for theme switching.

nodes.name: index.tsx (popup)
nodes.summary: Entry point for the popup page. Creates React root and renders the Popup component.

nodes.name: init (popup)
nodes.summary: Initializes the popup page React app by finding #app-container and rendering the Popup component.

nodes.name: SidePanel.tsx
nodes.summary: Side panel page component showing extension branding and a theme toggle button. Wrapped with error boundary and suspense.

nodes.name: SidePanel
nodes.summary: React component for the extension side panel. Displays logo and a ToggleButton that calls exampleThemeStorage.toggle.

nodes.name: index.tsx (side-panel)
nodes.summary: Entry point for the side panel page. Creates React root and renders the SidePanel component.

nodes.name: init (side-panel)
nodes.summary: Initializes the side panel page React app by finding #app-container and rendering the SidePanel component.

nodes.name: build-and-reload.ts
nodes.summary: Raycast command that builds the Chrome extension via the bridge HTTP API and then triggers a reload. Composes buildExtension() with a POST to /reload-extension.

nodes.name: command
nodes.summary: Default export async function. Calls buildExtension(), then POSTs to /reload-extension on the bridge HTTP server. Shows toast notifications for success or failure.

nodes.name: build-extension.ts
nodes.summary: Raycast command and reusable helper to trigger an extension build via the bridge. Exports buildExtension() which POSTs to /dev/build and a default command() wrapper.

nodes.name: buildExtension
nodes.summary: POSTs to BRIDGE_HTTP_URL/dev/build, shows animated toast while building, returns boolean indicating success. Used by build-and-reload command.

nodes.name: command
nodes.summary: Default export Raycast command entry point that simply calls buildExtension().

nodes.name: open-options.ts
nodes.summary: Raycast command that opens the extension options page by POSTing to the bridge /execute endpoint with commandId 'open-options'.

nodes.name: OpenOptions
nodes.summary: Default export async function. Sends POST to bridge /execute with {commandId: 'open-options'}, shows HUD on success or toast on failure.

nodes.name: reload-extension.ts
nodes.summary: Raycast command to reload the Chrome extension by POSTing to the bridge /reload-extension endpoint. Standalone reload without triggering a build.

nodes.name: reloadExtension
nodes.summary: Default export async function. POSTs to BRIDGE_HTTP_URL/reload-extension, shows success/failure toast.

nodes.name: run-command.tsx
nodes.summary: Main Raycast command list UI. Fetches available commands from the bridge (or falls back to a cached JSON file), renders them in a Raycast List with static and dynamic sections, and executes them via the bridge /execute endpoint.

nodes.name: fetchCommands
nodes.summary: Fetches the command list from bridge GET /commands. Falls back to reading a cached commands.json file from the DATA_DIR if the bridge is offline. Returns FetchResult with commands, sync state, and connection status.

nodes.name: runCommand
nodes.summary: Executes a command by ID. For static commands (open-options, search-bookmarks), launches the dedicated Raycast command. For dynamic commands, POSTs to bridge /execute endpoint.

nodes.name: getCommandIcon
nodes.summary: Maps command IDs to Raycast Icon values (Gear for open-options, Moon for toggle-theme, Terminal as default).

nodes.name: getStatusSubtitle
nodes.summary: Returns a human-readable status string based on bridge/Chrome connection state. Shows 'Live', 'Bridge online, Chrome disconnected', or cached timestamp.

nodes.name: RunCommand
nodes.summary: Default export React component. Renders a Raycast List with static and dynamic command sections. Uses fetchCommands on mount and runCommand on action.

nodes.name: search-bookmarks.tsx
nodes.summary: Raycast command that provides live search of Chrome bookmarks via the bridge /search-bookmarks endpoint. Debounces queries at 300ms, supports abort, and renders results with favicons.

nodes.name: faviconUrl
nodes.summary: Generates a Google favicon URL for a given page URL by extracting the hostname.

nodes.name: SearchBookmarks
nodes.summary: Default export React component. Renders a Raycast List with debounced search. Fetches bookmarks from bridge GET /search-bookmarks?q=... with abort support. Shows results with favicon, URL, and folder tag.

nodes.name: wdio.browser.conf.ts
nodes.summary: Browser-specific WebdriverIO configuration that loads the bundled extension, sets up Chrome/Firefox capabilities, and registers the getExtensionPath custom command.

nodes.name: wdio.conf.ts
nodes.summary: Base WebdriverIO configuration with Mocha framework, BDD UI, spec reporter, and shared timeout/retry settings for all e2e tests.

nodes.name: theme.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test helper that verifies theme toggle functionality by clicking a toggle button and checking the dark class on the .App wrapper, then toggling back.

nodes.name: canSwitchTheme
nodes.summary: Async helper that verifies the theme toggle button works by clicking it, checking the dark class toggles on/off the .App element, then restoring the initial state.

nodes.name: page-content-runtime.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test for the content-runtime page. Skips on Chrome (content scripts not allowed on extension pages). Opens the popup, clicks 'Content Script' button, and asserts the runtime content view root element exists.

nodes.name: extension-path.ts
nodes.summary: Utility module providing functions to resolve Chrome and Firefox extension base URLs by extracting the extension ID/UUID from browser internal pages.

nodes.name: getChromeExtensionPath
nodes.summary: Navigates to chrome://extensions, traverses shadow DOM to find the extension item, extracts its ID, and returns the chrome-extension:// base URL.

nodes.name: getFirefoxExtensionPath
nodes.summary: Navigates to about:debugging, extracts the internal UUID from the debugging page, and returns the moz-extension:// base URL.

nodes.name: page-content-ui.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test verifying the content UI shadow DOM div is injected on page load. Navigates to example.com and waits for the content-view-root element.

nodes.name: page-content.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test verifying the content script injects successfully on page load. Navigates to example.com and checks the content-view-root element exists.

nodes.name: page-dev-tools.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test for DevTools panel page. Verifies the panel loads with correct title and supports theme switching.

nodes.name: page-new-tab.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test for the new tab override page. Verifies the .App div renders and theme switching works. Supports both Chrome and Firefox URL schemes.

nodes.name: page-options.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test for the options page with three test cases: page accessibility, sidebar/navigation rendering (Redirector, Organizer, Bulk Actions buttons), and tab navigation between views.

nodes.name: page-popup.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test for the popup page. Verifies it opens with the correct title and supports theme switching.

nodes.name: page-side-panel.test.ts
nodes.summary: E2E test for the side panel page. Verifies it loads with correct title and supports theme switching.

nodes.name: smoke.test.ts
nodes.summary: Basic smoke test that verifies the browser can navigate to example.com and the page title is correct. Sanity check for the test infrastructure.

layers.name: Infrastructure & Tooling
layers.description: Build tooling, environment configuration, and developer utility scripts that support the monorepo development workflow.

layers.name: Core Shared Libraries
layers.description: Shared protocol definitions, HMR support, i18n, storage abstractions, and module management used across the extension and bridge.

layers.name: Bridge Server
layers.description: Standalone WebSocket and HTTP bridge server that enables communication between dev tools, the extension, and external integrations like Raycast.

layers.name: UI Framework
layers.description: Shared UI component library and utility functions providing consistent styling and components across all extension pages.

layers.name: Extension Pages
layers.description: Chrome/Firefox extension entry points and page implementations including popup, options, new-tab, devtools, content scripts, and side panel.

layers.name: Raycast Integration
layers.description: Raycast extension commands that interact with the bridge server to control browser tabs, reload extensions, and manage the bridge lifecycle.

layers.name: Testing
layers.description: End-to-end tests using WebdriverIO that validate extension pages, content scripts, and UI interactions across browsers.

tour.title: Bridge Server Entry Point
tour.description: The bridge server starts here, creating a combined HTTP and WebSocket server on localhost. It writes a PID file for process management, wires up route handlers, manages WebSocket connections, and implements graceful shutdown on SIGINT/SIGTERM. This is the central hub that enables dev tooling and external integrations to communicate with the browser extension.

tour.title: Bridge Routes & WebSocket Handlers
tour.description: The bridge exposes HTTP routes for health checks, tab listing, and command execution, while WebSocket handlers manage real-time connections with the extension. The routes module organizes Express-like request handling, and the WebSocket handler tracks connected browser extension clients for bidirectional messaging.

tour.title: Chrome DevTools Protocol Integration
tour.description: The bridge can reload extensions programmatically via Chrome DevTools Protocol (CDP). It discovers browser debug targets, finds or opens the extensions page, and evaluates chrome.developerPrivate.reload() through a CDP WebSocket connection. The state module tracks bridge runtime state for coordinating these operations.

tour.title: Shared Protocol Package
tour.description: The protocol package defines the shared contract between the bridge server, extension, and Raycast integration. It exports constants like BRIDGE_PORT and CDP_PORT, message type enumerations, and TypeScript interfaces for all commands. Every component that communicates over the bridge imports these protocol definitions to ensure type-safe messaging.

tour.title: Storage Layer & State Management
tour.description: The storage package provides a type-safe abstraction over chrome.storage with support for both local and session storage areas. It includes reactive hooks (useStorage, useStorageSuspense), a base storage class with change listeners, and specialized stores for theme, redirect settings, and extension state. This layer bridges the gap between raw browser storage APIs and the React UI components.

tour.title: Shared Utilities & HMR
tour.description: The shared package provides cross-cutting utilities including a toggle-theme helper, type guards, and manifest parsing used across all pages. The HMR package implements custom Hot Module Replacement for Chrome extensions, handling the complex reload lifecycle where standard Vite HMR cannot reach content scripts or background workers.

tour.title: Internationalization (i18n)
tour.description: The i18n package provides type-safe internationalization with support for Chrome extension message format and dynamic locale detection. It exports a t() translation function, language type definitions, and dev-mode utilities for testing translations outside the extension context. All UI-facing pages use this for user-visible strings.

tour.title: UI Component Library
tour.description: The UI package provides a shared component library built with Tailwind CSS, including pre-configured shadcn/ui components (Button, Input, etc.), a theme provider with dark/light mode support, and a utility function for className merging. All extension pages import from this package to maintain consistent styling.

tour.title: Extension Pages: Options & Popup
tour.description: The options page is the most complex UI, implementing a tabbed interface with Sidebar navigation for managing redirect rules, organizing tabs, and bulk actions. The popup provides quick access to theme toggling, extension status, and a link to options. Both pages compose shared UI components with storage-backed state for persistent settings.

tour.title: Content Scripts & DevTools
tour.description: Content scripts inject UI into web pages via shadow DOM (content-ui) or run background logic (content-runtime). The devtools integration registers a custom panel accessible from Chrome DevTools. These components demonstrate the extension's ability to interact with arbitrary web pages and provide developer inspection tools.

tour.title: Chrome Extension Manifest & Background
tour.description: The chrome-extension directory contains the Manifest V3 configuration and the background service worker. The manifest declares permissions, content scripts, and page overrides. The background script initializes the extension, sets up message listeners, and coordinates with the bridge server for development features like HMR reloading.

tour.title: Raycast Integration
tour.description: The Raycast extension provides macOS command palette commands to control the browser extension remotely via the bridge server. Commands include building and reloading the extension, running arbitrary commands, searching bookmarks, and opening the options page. Each command imports protocol constants and communicates with the bridge HTTP/WebSocket API.

tour.title: End-to-End Testing
tour.description: The tests directory contains WebdriverIO-based e2e tests that validate extension pages load correctly across Chrome and Firefox. Test helpers abstract browser-specific URL schemes and theme switching verification. The test suite covers popup, options, new-tab, content scripts, devtools panel, and side panel pages.
